artifacts.title: Seen receipt from 2022-12-04
artifacts.excerpt: Rarely seen someone hit it more nail on the head than @machineiv. Restricting AI to big conglomerates AND lessen the legal path to fair use, is bad. And we have to question why it's always big artists, the Karla Ortiz or the RJ Palmer, who push for those "solutions." https://t.co/JzcxhrOG6d

artifacts.title: Artists receipt from 2022-08-13
artifacts.excerpt: Top 20 dead artists used in @StableDiffusion Discord bot queries as of today, 1-2 PM, among the 773 artists in the collective archive of @sureailabs, @proximasan, @KyrickYoung and @EErratica. Can't wait for the Disco Diffusion 4000+ artists to be added ! https://t.co/DuqA2bo7QC

artifacts.title: Artists receipt from 2022-08-13
artifacts.excerpt: Still with my cursory glance at @sureailabs, @proximasan,  @KyrickYoung and @EErratica's database, here's the 20 most popular descriptive tags for artists - by artists on the database, and by number of hits on the @StableDiffusion, today between 1 and 2 PM CEST ! Any surprises ? https://t.co/rpCRQIBZF2
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: artists, kortizart, which, dead.

artifacts.title: Artists receipt from 2022-12-15
artifacts.excerpt: I firmly, fervently advocate for #AIartists (#AIIA, #AiArt, et al.), to actually embrace the #noaiart in their work. Not as a way to fuel further spite, but to actually address the genuine concerns of artists. Computers couldn't cope with this movement. But we can https://t.co/5YOOIk749M

artifacts.title: Artists receipt from 2022-09-08

planets.name: Kortizart
planets.summary: Archive cluster around kortizart: 50 posts; 4 quote-backed receipts pinned.

artifacts.title: Kortizart receipt from 2022-09-08
artifacts.excerpt: @kortizart But the fact you're willing to levy that as a way for me not to have a right to oppose you, or being mistaken when it's precisely what I have been trained and schooled to do, evidences why I keep this debate up. Saying someone is "not a artist" is an efficient way to disregard
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: kortizart, which, images_ai, danielpinalarts.

artifacts.title: Kortizart receipt from 2022-09-08
artifacts.excerpt: @kortizart Mmmh, depends where how you formulate the process  ☺️ AI gets you from a field of pixel to an often wavering synthesis of forms. You can stop there (just like you could snap sthg randomly with an iPhone and submit it to a contest). Or do more, before or after, concept or editing
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: kortizart, which, images_ai, danielpinalarts.

artifacts.title: Kortizart receipt from 2022-09-09
artifacts.excerpt: Let's be extremely clear about 3 things. 1) Don't condone any disparaging comment towards @kortizart, the person jumping in is not a friend or follow of mine. https://t.co/uCXSptUGGD
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: kortizart, which, images_ai, danielpinalarts.

artifacts.title: Kortizart receipt from 2022-09-08
artifacts.excerpt: @kortizart Ultimately, decoupling the tangible threat of automation and the ontological one of authorship shows how preventable those 2 specters are: theory + other media altered our views on authorship way before AI, and historical analogs already give a framework for the societal part
